Japanese: あなたにとって、綾里　真宵は 特別な存在だったはずです！
English: There's a very good reason why Maya Fey's life is so precious to you!

Japanese: なにしろ‥‥
English: After all...

Japanese: 綾里　千尋の、たったひとりの 妹だったのですからね。
English: She is Mia Fey's only sister.

Japanese: アヤサト　チヒロ‥‥
English: Mia... Fey.

Japanese: ‥‥千尋さんは、 あなたの後輩でした。
English: You once worked alongside her.

Japanese: あなた自身が‥‥まだ、 弁護士だったころのことです。
English: That was when you... were a defense attorney.

Japanese: ちょ‥‥ちょっと 待ってください！
English: W-Wait a second here!

Japanese: ゴドー検事が‥‥ 弁護士、ですって！
English: Mr. Godot is... is a defense attorney!?

Japanese: さすがの裁判長も お気づきだと思いますが‥‥
English: With Your Honor's piercing intellect, you must have figured it out by now...

Japanese: 《ゴドー》というのは、 彼の本名ではありません。
English: The real name of this man who calls himself "Godot"...

Japanese: 本当の名前は‥‥神乃木　荘龍。 そうですね？
English: His real name is... Diego Armando. Isn't that right?

Japanese: ‥‥最後にその名前で 呼ばれたのは‥‥
English: The last time someone called me by that name...

Japanese: もう、６年以上も前のコトだ。
English: ...was over 6 years ago.

Japanese: “かみのぎ　そうりゅう”‥‥ どこかで、聞いたことがあります。
English: Diego Armando... That name rings a bell...

Japanese: すべては‥‥ある審理から 始まりました。
English: It should, Your Honor. All of this is related to a single case...

Japanese: ‥‥脱獄犯・尾並田　美散が 服毒自殺をした、あの審理から‥‥
English: A case in which a convict named Terry Fawles killed himself...

Japanese: 初めての法廷で、綾里　千尋は ココロに深い傷を負いました。
English: Mia Fey's first time in court. The tragic outcome left a deep wound in her heart.

Japanese: ‥‥ある女の、 あまりに邪悪な犯行‥‥
English: She knew that behind it all was a heartless, scheming demoness in disguise.

Japanese: 千尋さんは、それを暴くことが できなかったのです。
English: But in the end, Mia couldn't tear off that disguise.

Japanese: ‥‥そんな彼女に、 手をさしのべた人物がいました。
English: However, there was one man who reached out to help her.

Japanese: ‥‥神乃木　荘龍。彼女の先輩に あたる、弁護士でした。
English: ...Diego Armando, a senior defense lawyer at the office where Mia worked.

Japanese: 私のせいです！　‥‥私のせいで、 尾並田さんは‥‥
English: It's my fault! It's all my fault that Mr. Fawles killed himself!

Japanese: チヒロ‥‥
English: Mia...

Japanese: 今はまだ、泣くときじゃねえ。
English: You can't cry yet.

Japanese: オトコが泣いていいのは‥‥ すべてを終えたときだけ、だぜ。
English: The only time a lawyer can cry is when it's all over.

Japanese: オレは、彼女に‥‥ ひかれていたんだろうぜ。
English: I was moved by her...

Japanese: ひたむきに依頼人を信じる、 純粋な、そのココロに‥‥
English: The way she put all her faith in her clients... That pure, sweet heart of hers...

Japanese: だから‥‥美柳　ちなみを どうしてもユルせなかった。
English: That's why... I could never forgive Dahlia Hawthorne.

Japanese: オレとチヒロは、あの誘拐事件を テッテイ的に調べなおした。
English: Mia and I thoroughly investigated that fake kidnapping incident.

Japanese: そして‥‥あの、運命の日。 美柳　ちなみを呼び出したんだ。
English: Then one fateful day, Dahlia wanted to meet with me...

Japanese: 審理から、半年後‥‥ 裁判所のカフェテリアに。
English: It had been 6 months since the trial... We met in the courthouse cafeteria.

Japanese: あ‥‥ッ！ お‥‥思い出しました！
English: Ah...! I just remembered!

Japanese: あなたは、６年前！ 裁判所で、毒を盛られた‥‥！
English: 6 years ago! Right here in this courthouse! You were poisoned...!

Japanese: ‥‥さすがのオレも、 予想していなかったぜ。
English: Even I didn't see it coming.

Japanese: 美柳　ちなみが‥‥オレの コーヒーに、毒を盛るなんて‥‥
English: Dahlia Hawthorne slipped some poison into my coffee.

Japanese: ‥‥一部の新聞記事は、 《殺人事件》と報道しましたな。
English: Some newspapers at the time called it a murder.

Japanese: マスコミに流された情報は、 かなり制限されていましたから‥‥
English: But very little information about the case was released to the press...

Japanese: しかし‥‥あなたは、 死んではいなかった！
English: But you weren't dead at all!

Japanese: ‥‥公式資料のどこにも、 《殺人事件》とは書かれていねえ。
English: No official reports ever actually called it a murder.

Japanese: オレは、ただ‥‥‥ 深い眠りについたんだ。
English: I was just in a deep, deep coma...
I see...

Japanese: ‥‥ずいぶん長いあいだ、 オレはムダに眠りつづけた。
English: ...My body shut down, and my life became nothing but a long, deep sleep.

Japanese: あの女の毒は、オレの神経中枢を ズタズタにむしばんだのさ。
English: That woman's poison did a real number on my central nervous system...

Japanese: 視力は失われて‥‥ 髪の色も、二度と戻らなかった。
English: I lost my sight, and all of my hair turned white due to the damage it caused.

Japanese: ‥‥検事さん‥‥
English: That's terrible...

Japanese: ‥‥オレが意識を取り戻したのは、 まさに奇跡だったらしい。
English: Apparently it was a miracle that I ever regained consciousness.

Japanese: 毒を飲まされてから‥‥ すでに５年の月日が流れていた。
English: Five years had passed since I drank that poisoned brew...

Japanese: ‥‥ある朝、オレは目をさました。 医者のいれたコーヒーのニオイで。
English: Then one morning, my eyes flew open... from the smell of a doctor's cup of coffee.

Japanese: ご‥‥５年間‥‥ あなたは、眠りつづけたのですか！
English: F-Five years...!? You were asleep for 5 years!?

Japanese: ‥‥オレを待っていたのは、 絶望的な知らせだった。
English: And the worst possible news was waiting for me...

Japanese: ‥‥綾里　千尋の、死‥‥
English: ...Mia Fey was dead.

Japanese: 目ざめた瞬間‥‥オレは、 すべてを失っていたのさ。
English: From the very moment I opened my eyes... I had already lost everything I thought I had.

Japanese: ‥‥愛した女は、 すでに殺害されて‥‥
English: The woman I loved had been murdered...

Japanese: そして‥‥憎んだ女は、 すでに死刑判決を受けていた。
English: And the woman I loathed had been sentenced to death.

Japanese: “憎んだ女”‥‥
English: The woman you loathed...?

Japanese: オレに、あのアツい１杯をおごって くれた女‥‥美柳　ちなみさ！
English: The woman who had spiked my scalding hot coffee... Dahlia Hawthorne!

Japanese: クッ‥‥！ さすが、チヒロだぜ。
English: Ha...! Good ol' Mia. She didn't let me down.

Japanese: 自分が殺される前に、キッチリ 復讐を済ませちまっていた。
English: She got her revenge before she checked out.

Japanese: ‥‥そう。
English: In the end...

Japanese: オレの目ざめを待つ者は‥‥ だれもいなかったのさ。
English: There wasn't anyone waiting for me when I woke up.

Japanese: そ‥‥そんな‥‥
English: Th-That's so sad...

Japanese: お寝坊さんのオレに残された 《生きる目的》は‥‥
English: For someone like me... For someone who had slept away their best days...

Japanese: もう、たった２つしかなかった。
English: ...there were only 2 reasons left to live.

Japanese: そのために‥‥オレは、 検事になることにしたのさ。
English: And it was for those 2 reasons that I decided to become a prosecutor.

Japanese: ２つの‥‥《生きる目的》‥‥？
English: If I may ask... What were your 2 reasons to live...?

Japanese: １つ目は、アンタだ。 ‥‥まるほどう。
English: The first was you, Trite.

Japanese: え‥‥！ ぼ‥‥ぼく、ですか‥‥！
English: Huh...!? M... M-Me?

Japanese: ‥‥もし、くだらねえ毒で 眠らされていなかったら‥‥
English: If I hadn't drank that stupid poison...

Japanese: オレは決して、綾里　千尋を 死なせはしなかった。
English: ...Mia Fey never would have died, much less the way she did.

Japanese: アンタが‥‥アンタだけが！ あのとき、彼女を守れたんだ。
English: You were the only one who was there to protect her!

Japanese: だが‥‥‥彼女は死んだ。 アンタがガキだったせいで。
English: But you let her die. It was all your fault.

Japanese: そ、そんな‥‥
English: I... It wasn't like that...

Japanese: オレは、この手で‥‥ アンタの力を試したかったのさ。
English: I wanted to see for myself what kind of a man you really were.

Japanese: そ‥‥そのために‥‥ あなたは、検事に？
English: S-So that's why you became a prosecutor?

Japanese: ‥‥もう１つ、理由があった。 モチロン‥‥“綾里　真宵”さ。
English: My other reason for living... She goes by the name of Maya Fey.

Japanese: あ‥‥あたし、ですか‥‥
English: Huh? You mean me...?

Japanese: チヒロを守れなかったオレの、 せめてもの罪ほろぼしさ。
English: You were the only way I could make up for the sin of not saving Mia.

Japanese: ‥‥１年前。倉院の里の 事件が解決されたとき‥‥
English: One year ago, when the Kurain Village incident was resolved...

Japanese: 綾里　キミ子が、何かを たくらむのは、明白だった。
English: ...it was obvious that Morgan Fey was planning something.

Japanese: その計画がどんなものだろうと‥‥ オレは、阻止するつもりでいた。
English: Whatever her evil plan was, I was determined to stop it.

Japanese: ‥‥《検事》という立場は、 その目的にピッタリだったのさ。
English: My role as a prosecutor put me in the perfect position to do something about her.

Japanese: そして‥‥春美ちゃんとの面会を 盗聴したわけですか。
English: That's how you overheard Pearls's visit with Morgan at the detention center.

Japanese: “その時”が近づいているのは わかっていた。
English: I knew that the time was drawing near.

Japanese: 《計画書》のありかを知ったオレは ‥‥先回りをした。
English: Since I knew the plan, I thought I could foil it.

Japanese: あの計画の、ウラをかくために。 それができれば‥‥
English: My goal was to outwit the plan. I thought if I could do that...

Japanese: ‥‥もう、あの少女が計画に 利用されるコトもないはずだ。
English: ...I could keep that girl from being caught up in it.

Japanese: （‥‥たしかに‥‥）
English: (That makes sense...)

Japanese: （計画の真意が、真宵ちゃんの 暗殺だと思い知らされれば‥‥）
English: (If Pearls had known that the actual purpose of the plan was to kill Maya...)

Japanese: （春美ちゃんは、もう二度と 協力しないだろう）
English: (...she never would have helped out.)

Japanese: ‥‥いよいよ犯行の日が 近づいたとき‥‥
English: Finally, the day of the plan was drawing near...

Japanese: オレは、ふたりの協力者たちと 連絡をとった。
English: I contacted both of my accomplices.
Accomplices...?

Japanese: 葉桜院の尼僧、あやめと‥‥ 綾里　舞子‥‥だ。
English: Iris of Hazakura Temple and... Misty Fey.

Japanese: 特にあやめは、計画書によると “罪を着せられる”立場だった。
English: I especially needed the help of Iris...

Japanese: ‥‥彼女の協力は、 ゼッタイに必要だったのさ。
English: She was to take the fall in my back-up plan in case we couldn't control Pearl Fey.

Japanese: で、でも‥‥！
English: B-But...!

Japanese: どうして、おかあさんを 知っていたんですか！
English: How did you contact my mother!?

Japanese: もう‥‥２０年近くも 行方不明だったハズなのに‥‥！
English: She had been missing for almost 20 years!

Japanese: 表向きには、な‥‥
English: Officially, yes...

Japanese: ど、どういうことですか！ ‥‥《表向き》‥‥？
English: What!? What do you mean, officially!?

Japanese: アンタも、聞いたことは あるだろう。
English: You've heard about it, haven't you?

Japanese: かつての倉院流霊媒道と、政財界の 結びつきの強さを‥‥
English: About the strong ties between the main family and the government...

Japanese: （そういえば‥‥ビキニさんが 言ってたっけ‥‥）
English: (Now that you mention it... Bikini did say something to that effect...)

Japanese: （倉院流・家元の権力は、 絶大だった、って‥‥）
English: (She said that the Master of Kurain had great authority...)

Japanese: ‥‥権威は失われても、 その力はホンモノだ。
English: Even without her official position, Misty Fey still wielded great influence...

Japanese: 警察は‥‥つねに、綾里　舞子の 動きを、マークしていたのさ。
English: The police have been keeping an eye on her movements all this time.

Japanese: だから‥‥オレは、 彼女に接触することができた。
English: That's how I was able to contact her...

Japanese: 《検事》という、 この身分のおかげで、な。
English: Again, because of my position as a prosecutor.

Japanese: おかあさんは‥‥検事さんと 協力していたんですね‥‥
English: So my mother... was cooperating with you?

Japanese: ‥‥忘れるな。
English: Don't ever forget...

Japanese: どんなに離れていても、彼女は 常に、アンタのことを考えていた。
English: ...no matter how far away from you she was, she never stopped thinking about you.

Japanese: ‥‥おかあさんが‥‥
English: Sh-She was always...

Japanese: だから‥‥アンタを守るためなら、 なんでもやったはずだ。
English: That's why... I knew she would do anything to protect you.

Japanese: その《カクゴ》は‥‥ 彼女のツエに、あらわれている。
English: If you want to know how strong her resolve to protect you was, look at her staff.

Japanese: カタナを仕込まれた‥‥ あのツエ、ですか！
English: Her staff...? The one with the sword in it?

Japanese: いよいよ、計画が実行される その日になって‥‥
English: The day the plan was to be carried out arrived soon enough...

Japanese: オレたちは、葉桜院で初めて カオを合わせた。
English: We met for the first time at Hazakura Temple.

Japanese: そのとき‥‥アンタの母親から あのツエを見せられて‥‥
English: That's when your mother showed me her special staff...

Japanese: ‥‥オレは、思い知らされた。 “娘を想うキモチ”ってヤツを‥‥
English: I realized it then... Just how far she was willing to go...

Japanese: あのカタナで、綾里　舞子は アンタを守るつもりだったのさ。
English: She was ready to use that sword to protect you from Morgan Fey, if necessary.

Japanese: ‥‥そう！ 自分の命にかえても‥‥
English: ...Yes! Even if it meant paying the ultimate price.

Japanese: お‥‥おかあさん‥‥
English: M-Mother...

Japanese: あの晩‥‥事件当夜。
English: That night... The night of the crime.

Japanese: 綾里　キミ子の計画をツブす ポイントは、たった１つだった。
English: There was just one way to stop Morgan's evil plan.

Japanese: モチロン‥‥ 綾里　春美、ですね。
English: You mean Pearls, don't you?

Japanese: あの子に《美柳　ちなみ》の霊を 霊媒させてはならない‥‥
English: We had to make sure she didn't channel Dahlia Hawthorne...

Japanese: ‥‥春美ちゃんは‥‥ 今晩は、どうするのかしら？
English: ...Well, Pearl? What are you going to do tonight?

Japanese: え‥‥ええと。そうですね‥‥
English: Well, umm...

Japanese: よかったら‥‥わたくしの お部屋に来ないかしら？
English: If you'd like, you can come to my room.

Japanese: いっしょに、 ご本を読んであげましょう。
English: Perhaps we can read some books together.

Japanese: ‥‥オレたちは、あの子の 足止めをしようと考えた。
English: We thought we could prevent her from playing her part in Morgan's plan.

Japanese: しかし‥‥‥ あの子は、来なかった。
English: But... She never showed up.

Japanese: ‥‥あたしのために、 《奥の院》へ来ちゃったから‥‥
English: She was worried and followed me to the Inner Temple...

Japanese: それは、考えられる サイアクの事態だった。
English: That was the thing we were most afraid of.

Japanese: だからこそ‥‥
English: And that's why...

Japanese: 綾里　舞子は、 霊媒せざるを得なかったのさ。
English: Misty Fey had to do the channeling herself.

Japanese: 《美柳　ちなみ》の霊を‥‥ 自分自身の身体に。
English: She channeled Dahlia Hawthorne into her own body.

Japanese: ど‥‥どういうコトですか！
English: What...!? What do you mean!?

Japanese: ‥‥彼女が、先に 霊を呼んでしまえば‥‥
English: If she channeled the spirit first...

Japanese: 春美ちゃんには、もう どうすることもできないのです。
English: ...then Pearls wouldn't be able to do it herself.

Japanese: 綾里　舞子‥‥ 《家元》の霊力は、ゼッタイだ。
English: As Master of Kurain, Misty Fey's power was supreme.

Japanese: ‥‥そう。
English: So that's how it went down.

Japanese: 彼女は、綾里　春美に霊媒させない ために、みずから霊を呼んだのさ！
English: She channeled Dahlia Hawthorne so that Pearl Fey wouldn't be able to!

Japanese: あ‥‥‥ あああああああああああッ！
English: Ah...! Whaaaaaaaat!?

Japanese: ‥‥な‥‥ なんということでしょう‥‥！
English: What...!? Is this true!?

Japanese: ‥‥オレは、なんとしても アンタを守るつもりでいた。
English: My role in the plan was to make sure no one was going to hurt Maya Fey.

Japanese: だから‥‥《奥の院》には、 オレ自身が身をひそめていたのさ。
English: That's why... I hid myself at the Inner Temple.

Japanese: “もしも”のときに‥‥ 《美柳　ちなみ》を止めるために。
English: Just in case you needed to be saved from Dahlia Hawthorne.

Japanese: ‥‥ゴドー検事‥‥
English: Godot...

Japanese: ‥‥さて。
English: Anyway...

Japanese: オレが認めるのは、ここまでだぜ。 ‥‥まるほどう。
English: That's all I'm going to admit to... Trite.
Huh...?

Japanese: たしかに、 アンタは優秀な弁護士だよ。
English: There's no doubt about it. You're a great defense lawyer.

Japanese: だが‥‥ ただ、それだけだったのさ。
English: But... you're going to have to do the rest yourself.

Japanese: ‥‥この事件が起こるに至った 背景は、ハッキリしました。
English: The background leading up to this incident has been laid bare.

Japanese: 残された問題は‥‥ただ１つです。 『だれが、被害者を殺害したのか』
English: There's just one question remaining, Mr. Wright. "Who killed the victim?"

Japanese: 犯行が可能だったのは‥‥ たった、ふたりです。
English: There are only two possible suspects right now.

Japanese: ‥‥綾里　真宵さん‥‥
English: Maya Fey...

Japanese: もしくは、ゴドー検事！ ‥‥あなたです。
English: And I'm sad to say... you, Mr. Godot!

Japanese: ‥‥なあ、まるほどう。
English: Well, Trite...

Japanese: もし、アンタが “ホンモノ”ならば‥‥
English: If you're the real deal...

Japanese: 最後に、立証してみせてくれよ。
English: ...then finish this thing once and for all.

Japanese: だれがやったのか‥‥カンペキに！ アンタ自身の手でなァ！
English: Show us beyond a shadow of a doubt that you can finish this on your own!

Japanese: な‥‥なるほどくん！ 待って‥‥おねがい！
English: N-No, Nick...! P-Please don't!

Japanese: ま‥‥真宵ちゃん‥‥
English: M... Maya...

Japanese: あたし‥‥ゼンブ、聞いたの。
English: I... I heard the whole thing.

Japanese: お姉ちゃんから、さっき。 医務室で‥‥！
English: From my sister... In the medical office...!

Japanese: だから！　あたし‥‥ 検事さんを守らなきゃ、って‥‥
English: That's why! That's why... I have to protect Mr. Godot...

Japanese: あたし‥‥できないよ。 ‥‥証言なんて‥‥
English: I can't do it... I can't testify against him...

Japanese: だって‥‥
English: After all...

Japanese: お姉ちゃんと、あたしのために‥‥ 命をかけてくれたヒトなんだよ！
English: ...he's the man who put his life on the line to protect Mia... and me, too!

Japanese: ‥‥そんなコト、わかってるよ！
English: Maya... I know that!

Japanese: な、なるほどくん‥‥！
English: N-Nick...

Japanese: でも‥‥
English: But even so...

Japanese: だからと言って、その《罪》が 消えるわけじゃない！
English: It doesn't absolve him of his crime!

Japanese: ‥‥証言をしてもらうよ、 真宵ちゃん。
English: ...Please, Maya. Testify.

Japanese: 綾里　真宵さん。 ‥‥おねがいします。
English: Ms. Fey. Your testimony please.

Japanese: これが、最後の証言だよ。
English: This is the final testimony.

Japanese: かくそうとしても、ムダだ。 ぼくは、かならず見つけだす。
English: Don't bother trying to hide anything, because I'll know.

Japanese: きみのコトバから‥‥《真実》を。
English: I want to hear the truth from your own lips...

Japanese: ‥‥わかりました。
English: I understand.

Japanese: きっと‥‥それがイチバン いいんだね。なるほどくん‥‥
English: I'm sure you're right... I'm ready now, Nick...

Japanese: 犯行の瞬間‥‥。あなたが 気を失うまでの、その一瞬‥‥
English: Alright, young lady... Tell us about the moments before you lost consciousness.

Japanese: いったい、何が起こったか‥‥？ 聞かせていただきましょう！
English: What exactly happened at the time of the murder!?

Japanese: ～事件当夜・犯行の瞬間～
English: -- The Time of the Murder --

Japanese: あたし‥‥あのとき、赤い光を 見たような気がしたんです。
English: ...Just before it happened, I think I saw some red lights. Three of them.

Japanese: 助けを求めようとしたら‥‥ 血がふりかかってきました！
English: I thought I'd ask for help, but... just then I was splattered with blood!

Japanese: でも‥‥《彼女》は死ななかった。 背後の《敵》に、反撃したんです。
English: She wasn't dead though... And she struck back at the enemy behind her.

Japanese: 突然、赤い光が消えて‥‥ あたりは、闇に包まれました。
English: Suddenly, the red lights went out and the whole area was dark.

Japanese: ‥‥そして、その瞬間！ 恐ろしい悲鳴が聞こえたんです！
English: ...Just at that moment, there was a horrible scream!

Japanese: やがて‥‥美柳　ちなみは倒れて、 あたしも意識を失いました‥‥
English: Right after that... Dahlia collapsed and I lost consciousness.

Japanese: 《赤い光》‥‥ハッキリ おぼえていないのですか！
English: These red lights... I thought you said you don't remember seeing them!

Japanese: は、はい‥‥。
English: I-I'm sorry...

Japanese: たぶん、見たと思うんですけど。 すぐに、見えなくなったから‥‥
English: I thought I saw them, but then they disappeared all of a sudden...

Japanese: クッ‥‥！ ザンネンだったな、まるほどう‥‥
English: Ha...! Things break, Trite... Even the best of theories.

Japanese: 綾里　舞子を刺したのが、だれか？ ‥‥まだ、立証できねえぜ。
English: Who was it that stabbed Misty Fey? It looks like you still can't prove it.

Japanese: （今度こそ、真宵ちゃんは 真実を証言している‥‥）
English: (Maya is telling the truth this time, I know it...)

Japanese: （‥‥これから先は、 ぼくのシゴトだ！）
English: (The rest is up to me!)

Japanese: ‥‥それでは、弁護人！
English: ...Well then, Mr. Wright!

Japanese: この証人に対する、 最後の尋問をおねがいします！
English: Proceed with your final cross-examination of the witness!

Japanese: ～事件当夜・犯行の瞬間～
English: -- The Time of the Murder --

Japanese: あたし‥‥あのとき、赤い光を 見たような気がしたんです。
English: ...Just before it happened, I think I saw some red lights. Three of them.

Japanese: 助けを求めようとしたら‥‥ 血がふりかかってきました！
English: I thought I'd ask for help, but... just then I was splattered with blood!

Japanese: でも‥‥《彼女》は死ななかった。 背後の《敵》に、反撃したんです。
English: She wasn't dead though... And she struck back at the enemy behind her.

Japanese: 突然、赤い光は消えて‥‥ あたりは、闇に包まれました。
English: Suddenly, the red lights went out and the whole area was dark.

Japanese: ‥‥そして、その瞬間！ 恐ろしい悲鳴が聞こえたんです！
English: ...Just at that moment, there was a horrible scream!

Japanese: やがて‥‥美柳　ちなみは倒れて、 あたしも意識を失いました‥‥
English: Right after that... Dahlia collapsed and I lost consciousness.

Japanese: （この証言で‥‥ ゴドー検事の犯行を立証する！）
English: (I've got to use this testimony to prove that Godot committed the murder!)

Japanese: （‥‥できれば、ぼくも こんなコトは、したくない‥‥）
English: (I really wish I didn't have to do it...)

Japanese: （でも‥‥、 なんとなく、感じるんだ）
English: (But for some reason...)

Japanese: （ゴドー検事自身も‥‥ それを望んでいる、って）
English: (I get the feeling that some part of Godot actually wants me to...)

Japanese: 闇の中で見た《光》だろう？ 見まちがうことはないと思うけど。
English: Lights shining in the dark? I don't think you could make a mistake about that.

Japanese: ‥‥うん。たぶん、 見たんだと思う。でも‥‥
English: ...Yeah, you're right. I think I saw them, but...

Japanese: やっぱり、ハッキリしないの。
English: I can't say for certain...

Japanese: それが、ゴドー検事さんの マスクだったかどうか‥‥
English: And I can't tell you for sure they were from Mr. Godot's mask either...

Japanese: （‥‥今度こそ、かばっている ようすはないけど‥‥）
English: (I don't get the feeling she's covering for him anymore, but...)

Japanese: でも‥‥そのあと、きみは《光》に 向かって、助けを求めている。
English: But just after that, you turned towards the lights and called for help.

Japanese: その《光》が‥‥ゴドー検事だと 思ったからじゃないのかな？
English: Isn't that because you thought the lights were coming from Mr. Godot's mask?

Japanese: ‥‥ううん‥‥そうだね。
English: Yeah... I guess so.

Japanese: あのときは、そう思った ような気もするんだけど‥‥
English: Maybe that's what I was thinking at the time...

Japanese: やっぱり‥‥思い出せないの。 だって‥‥
English: But... I can't remember. After all...

Japanese: あたしの前には、姿の見えない アイツがいたんだから！
English: ...there was a person I couldn't identify in front of me!

Japanese: （‥‥真宵ちゃんには、目の前に 命のキケンが迫っていた）
English: (Maya's life was in terrible danger at the time.)

Japanese: （その状況で、ハッキリした コタエを聞くのは、ムリがある）
English: (There's no way she can remember the details of the scene perfectly.)

Japanese: ‥‥それでは、証言を つづけてください。
English: ...Alright then, let's go on with the testimony.

Japanese: 《赤い光》に気づいたあなたは どうしましたか？
English: What did you do when you saw the red lights?

Japanese: その血は‥‥やっぱり、その。 《美柳　ちなみ》の‥‥？
English: That blood... Was it Dahlia Hawthorne's blood...?

Japanese: たぶん、そうだと思う。 あの瞬間‥‥
English: I think so, probably. Just at that moment...

Japanese: ‥‥耳元で、小さく 悲鳴が聞こえたの。
English: ...I heard a soft scream that seemed to be close by.

Japanese: あれは‥‥女のヒトの声でした。
English: It was a woman's voice.

Japanese: そのとき《犯人》が、 凶器で背中から、刺した‥‥
English: So that was when the killer stabbed the victim from behind with the murder weapon...

Japanese: ‥‥そういうコトですかな。
English: Is that right?

Japanese: （どうやら‥‥やっぱり、 あぶないところだったようだ）
English: (Without a doubt, Maya was in the middle of a really dangerous situation...)

Japanese: とにかく‥‥被害者は 凶器で身体をつらぬかれた。
English: Anyway... the victim was stabbed by the murder weapon.

Japanese: それから、どうなったのかい？
English: What happened after that?

Japanese: は、反撃‥‥！ どうして、それがわかるの？
English: S-Struck back...!? How do you know that?

Japanese: たしかに‥‥ハッキリ 見えたわけじゃないけど‥‥
English: Well, you're right... I didn't see it exactly...

Japanese: 息づかいや、もみあう気配を 感じたから。
English: But I could tell by the sounds of their breathing and movement.

Japanese: それに‥‥血のニオイも。
English: And also, the smell of blood.

Japanese: ‥‥ヒドい目にあいましたな。
English: You have witnessed more horror than any young lady should ever have to in one life.

Japanese: それで‥‥あなた自身は、 どうしたのですかな？
English: So then, what did you do at that point?

Japanese: う‥‥動けませんでした。
English: I... I couldn't move.

Japanese: 暗がりの中に、２つの人影が うっすら見えただけで‥‥
English: I could just barely make out their shadows moving in the dark, but...

Japanese: あたし、どうしていいか わからなかったんです！
English: I had no idea what to do!

Japanese: そのとき‥‥例の《赤い光》は、 まだ見えていたのかな？
English: Could you still see the red lights you mentioned earlier?

Japanese: う‥‥うん。 見えていた、と思う。
English: Y-Yes. I-I think so.

Japanese: でも‥‥
English: But...
They went out?

Japanese: う‥‥うん。急に、 見えなくなっちゃったの。
English: Y-Yes. Suddenly I couldn't see them at all.

Japanese: ど、どういうことでしょう‥‥
English: Wh-What could it mean?

Japanese: （《赤い光》がゴドー検事の マスクだと考えると‥‥）
English: (If the red lights were coming from Godot's mask...)

Japanese: （争っている最中、その光が “消えた”ということは‥‥）
English: (And they went out right in the middle of a fight...)

Japanese: 《コワれちゃった》、って コトなのかな‥‥マスク。
English: Maybe the mask was damaged.

Japanese: （もしくは‥‥ マスクが《はずれた》か）
English: (Or maybe it was knocked off.)

Japanese: あるいは‥‥ 《電池が切れた》のかもしれねえ。
English: Or maybe... the batteries ran out. Right, Trite?

Japanese: 切れたのは、《赤いマメ電球》の ほうだったのかもしれませんな。
English: Or maybe those little red pin-head looking lights just stopped working.

Japanese: （‥‥とにかく、そのとき 何かが起こったんだ！）
English: (What could have really happened then!?)

Japanese: 『その瞬間』というのは‥‥
English: What do you mean by, "Just at that moment"?

Japanese: やっぱり、《赤い光が消えたとき》 ‥‥ということ？
English: Do you mean the moment when the red lights went out?

Japanese: ‥‥うん。そうだよ。
English: ...Yes, that's right.

Japanese: そのときの“悲鳴”は‥‥ 美柳　ちなみ、だったのかな。
English: The scream that you heard then, was it Dahlia Hawthorne?

Japanese: ちがうと思う。
English: I don't think so.

Japanese: あれは、たぶん‥‥
English: I'm pretty sure...

Japanese: ‥‥男のヒトの声、だったから。
English: ...it was a man's voice.

Japanese: な‥‥‥‥ なんですってェェェッ！
English: Wha-Wha-Wha... Whaaaaaaaat!?

Japanese: で、では！　その悲鳴は‥‥ 《犯人》のものですかッ！
English: S-So then, that scream came... from the killer!?

Japanese: ‥‥そういうことになります。
English: That's got to be it.

Japanese: ふ‥‥‥ふむう‥‥‥ッ！
English: Hmm... Hmmm... Hmmmm...!

Japanese: きっと‥‥
English: I think...

Japanese: 美柳　ちなみが、《犯人》の ツエを奪って反撃したんだね。
English: ...Dahlia Hawthorne must have taken the blade and attacked the killer with it.

Japanese: それで、思わず《犯人》は 悲鳴を上げたのでしょう。
English: And then the killer let out a scream of pain, huh?

Japanese: そのあと、ツエを奪い返して トドメを刺した‥‥のかな。
English: After that, the killer stole the blade back and delivered the final blow... I guess.

Japanese: ‥‥いかがですか、弁護人。
English: ...Well, Mr. Wright?

Japanese: こう考えれば、モンダイないと 思いますが‥‥
English: It seems to make sense to me...

Japanese: （今のスイリ‥‥もっともらしく 聞こえたけど‥‥どうだろう？）
English: (It sounds like a reasonable deduction, but I still kinda wonder...)

Japanese: おそらく、そのとおりの ことが起こったのでしょう。
English: I think that's exactly how it happened.

Japanese: ‥‥まあ、私のスイリは めったにハズれませんからな。
English: Of course. After all, my deductions are almost certainly always correct.

Japanese: ‥‥それでは、証言を つづけていただきましょう。
English: Alright, let's go on with the testimony.

Japanese: 犯人と被害者は、はげしく争った。 ‥‥それから、どうなりましたか？
English: The killer and the victim were fighting ferociously... What happened then?

Japanese: ザンネンながら‥‥
English: I'm sorry to say this...

Japanese: 今の説明には、 大きなムジュンがあります！
English: ...but that interpretation would create an enormous contradiction!

Japanese: ‥‥まあ、私のスイリは めったに当たりませんからな。
English: That makes sense. After all, my deductions are almost certainly never correct.

Japanese: 証言を、よく思い出してください。 犯人が悲鳴を上げるより《前》‥‥
English: Remember the testimony she just gave. Before the killer let out a scream...

Japanese: すでに証人は、被害者の血を 身体にあびているのです。
English: ...Maya said she had already been splattered by the victim's blood.

Japanese: つまり！　すでに、ツエは、 被害者の身体をつらぬいていた！
English: In other words, the blade in the staff had already been plunged into the victim!

Japanese: あ‥‥そ、そうか‥‥！
English: Ah...! Is-Is that right...!?

Japanese: ‥‥身体に刺さったツエを、 反撃に使えるワケがありません。
English: She couldn't have struck back with a sword that was already stuck in her body.

Japanese: 《犯人》に悲鳴を上げさせた “凶器”は‥‥
English: The weapon that caused the killer to let out a scream...

Japanese: ‥‥ツエとはベツに 存在したはずなのです！
English: ...must have been something other than the staff!

Japanese: それならば‥‥聞かせて もらおうか、まるほどう。
English: If you're so sure about that, then don't keep us waiting any longer, Trite.

Japanese: （あの状況で、“凶器”として 使えたモノは‥‥）
English: (There's only one thing I can think of...)

Japanese: （もう‥‥１つしか、 考えられない！）
English: (...that could have been used as the weapon here!)

Japanese: ‥‥ツエで身体をつらぬかれた 《美柳　ちなみ》は‥‥
English: If Dahlia Hawthorne had already been stabbed in the back by the staff...

Japanese: いったい、何を使って 《犯人》に反撃したのか‥‥？
English: ...what could she have used to strike back at the killer?

Japanese: いかがですか、裁判長！
English: Well, Your Honor? What do you think?

Japanese: 正直なところ‥‥
English: To be honest with you...

Japanese: それを使って、私自身が あなたに反撃したいところです。
English: I feel like using my gavel to strike back at you right now.
Huh...?

Japanese: やっぱり‥‥アンタは そのテイド、ってコトかい。
English: You're nothing but a joke. I knew you couldn't take it all the way.

Japanese: ぐううう‥‥ッ！
English: Uggggh...!

Japanese: （なんで、こんなところで まちがえるんだ‥‥）
English: (How could I mess up at a time like this...?)

Japanese: じゃあ‥‥美柳　ちなみを 刺した《犯人》は‥‥？
English: And what about the killer that stabbed Dahlia...?

Japanese: ‥‥うん。わからないの。
English: ...I'm sorry, I don't know.

Japanese: あ、あの！ ホントだよ、なるほどくん！
English: R-Really! It's true, Nick!

Japanese: ‥‥わかってるよ。
English: I believe you...

Japanese: （この局面で‥‥ 真宵ちゃんは、ウソはつかない）
English: (On this point, I'm sure Maya isn't lying.)

Japanese: クッ‥‥！　どうやら‥‥
English: Ha...! It appears...

Japanese: 《闇》にジャマされて いるみたいだな、まるほどう。
English: ...that the darkness has proven to be your undoing, Trite.

Japanese: ふむう‥‥‥
English: Hmm...
